---
title: "Brain Simulation Workshop"
toc: false
page-layout: full
body-class: workshop-home-page
title-block-banner: false
format: html
---

::: {.workshop-hero aria-labelledby="workshop-hero-title"}
<iframe class="workshop-hero__visual" src="brain-network-background.html" title="Animated brain network visualization" aria-hidden="true" tabindex="-1"></iframe>

::: {.workshop-hero__overlay aria-hidden="true"}
:::

::: {.workshop-hero__inner}
::: {.workshop-kicker}
[FAIR Brain Data Science Bootcamp](https://ebrains.eu/news-and-events/events/2026/fair-brain-data-science-bootcamp-2026) · Track 4
:::

# Mechanistic Whole-Brain Simulation Workshop {#workshop-hero-title}

::: {.workshop-hero__lead}
Personalized brain network modeling with The Virtual Brain, ontology-driven
model specification, and gradient-based optimization.
:::

::: {.workshop-hero__meta aria-label="Workshop details"}
[12-13 May 2026]{.workshop-hero__pill}
[Karolinska Institutet, Stockholm]{.workshop-hero__pill}
[Widerströmska huset]{.workshop-hero__pill}
:::

::: {.workshop-actions}
[Start Setup](setup.qmd){.workshop-action .workshop-action--primary}
[Open Slides](slides.qmd){.workshop-action}
[View Agenda](agenda/agenda.qmd){.workshop-action}
[Notebooks](notebooks/1_introduction.qmd){.workshop-action}
:::
:::
:::

::: {.home-section}
## About

Track 4 of the **FAIR Brain Data Science Bootcamp 2026** — a joint event by
[EBRAINS](https://ebrains.eu/news-and-events/events/2026/fair-brain-data-science-bootcamp-2026)
and [INCF](https://www.incf.org/incf-brain-data-science-bootcamp-2026), hosted
at **Karolinska Institutet**, Widerströmska huset
([Tomtebodavägen 18](https://maps.app.goo.gl/), Stockholm, Sweden).

The bootcamp brings together hands-on training across brain atlasing,
electrophysiology, data sharing, and computational modeling within the EBRAINS
research infrastructure. Track 4 focuses on personalized whole-brain network
modeling with [The Virtual Brain](https://thevirtualbrain.org), combining
individualized model definition, simulation, and analysis with ontology-driven
model specification ([TVB-O](https://virtual-twin.github.io/tvbo)) and
gradient-based optimization
([TVB-Optim](https://virtual-twin.github.io/tvboptim))
[@Martin2025; @Pille2025].

::: {.home-pillars}
::: {.home-pillar}
### Network structure

Brain regions, tractography, surface geometry, and anatomical constraints.
:::

::: {.home-pillar}
### Model dynamics

Neural mass equations, coupling functions, integration, and observations.
:::

::: {.home-pillar}
### Optimization loop

Simulation targets, differentiable workflows, and parameter fitting.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.home-section}
## Program

::: {.home-pathways}
::: {.home-pathway}
[01]{.home-pathway__number}

#### [Setup](setup.qmd)

Install `uv`, `tvb`, `tvbo`, and `tvboptim`. Verify the environment before
day one.
:::

::: {.home-pathway}
[02]{.home-pathway__number}

#### [Agenda](agenda/agenda.qmd)

Two-day program: lectures, hands-on sessions, and a mini hackathon.
:::

::: {.home-pathway}
[03]{.home-pathway__number}

#### [Slides](slides.qmd)

RevealJS slides used in the live sessions.
:::

::: {.home-pathway}
[04]{.home-pathway__number}

#### [Notebooks](notebooks/1_introduction.qmd)

Hands-on Jupyter notebooks: introduction, FAIR models, dynamics, optimization,
and stimulation.
:::
:::
:::

::: {.home-section}
## Quick start

```bash
pip install uv
uv pip install tvb tvbo tvboptim
```

Source code: <https://github.com/virtual-twin/Brain-Simulation-Workshop>.
:::

::: {.home-section}
## References {.unnumbered}

::: {#refs}
:::
:::
